LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JENNIFER MATTHEWS' MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16. IS CONTINUED FROM AUG-04-2014 TO AUGUST 25, 2014 TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE REPLY PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN AUGUST 4, 2014 AT 4:00 P.M. OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: JOHN STEWART, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. (302/JKS)
ORDER ON DEFENDANT JENNIFER MATHEWS' MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES PER CCP 425.16

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF PEDRUCO, ALBERTO TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT MATHEWS, JENNIFER ON THE COMPLAINT, SAID DEFENDANT TO HAVE JUDGMENT THEREON FOR $10,000.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $10,000.00, (FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES & COSTS) (SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JENNIFER MATHEWS' MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16 - THE COURT AWARDS $10,000 IN FEES FOR DEFENDANT PREVAILING ON THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION AND BRINGING THE INSTANT MOTION FOR FEES. DEFENDANT'S DECLARATION AND TIME RECORDS SUBSTANTIATE THE FEES AND THE CLAIMED FEES ARE REASONABLE. ORDER & JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302)

Off Calendar Jan-08-2015 Continued To Mar-30-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication, Of Plaintiff'S Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-24-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JONATHAN S. LARSEN of Sarrail, Castillo & Hall, LLP of (650) 685-9200 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JASON M. ERLICH of McCormack & Erlick, LLP of (415) 296-8420 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JENNIFER MATHEWS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication, Of Plaintiff'S Complaint is denied. Defendant fails to show that plaintiff cannot establish his libel cause of action. This case does not involve a public concern. Rather, the thrust of plaintiff's complaint to the human resources department related to personal items missing from her locker. Plaintiff does not need to prove malice. The substance of defendant's e-mail, e.g., that plaintiff was under investigation for gender discrimination, was false. (Fernandez Depo., 30:6-21). The statements were libel on their face and tended to injure plaintiff in his occupation. Therefore, plaintiff need not prove special damages. See Cal. Civil Code secs. 45 and 45a; Walker v. Kiousis (2001) 93 Cal.App.4th 1432, 1441-1442. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-04-2014 Continued To Jul-30-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Complaint Pursuant To Code Of Civ Proc 425.16;
Off Calendar May-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Larsen, Esq., 650.685.9200, for plaintiff Alberto Pedruco.
Jason Erlich, Esq., 415.296.8420, for defendant Jennifer Matthews.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT JENNIFER MATTHEWS' Special Motion To Strike Complaint Pursuant To Code Of Civ Proc 425.16 is granted, in part, and denied, in part. Defendant Jennifer Mathews has met her initial burden to show that she engaged in protected activity by sending the two emails pertaining to her complaint to Human Resources. (CCP 425.16(e).) Defendant's emails are not protected by the litigation privilege. (Civ. Code 47(b).) For the first cause of action for defamation, Plaintiff has met his shifting burden of showing a probability of prevailing of the merits. Defendant's own evidence demonstrates that the contents of the two emails were false; Defendant was specifically reprimanded by Human Resources for sending these emails. (Mathews Decl., Exhibits B - E.) The false and unprivileged publication of these emails to 200 members of the San Francisco Division of the United Fire Service Women's Organization has a tendency to injure Plaintiff in his occupation. (Civ. Code 45.) For the second cause of action, Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden of showing a probability of prevailing on the merits. Plaintiff has not proffered any evidence to support his intentional infliction of emotional distress claims. (See Pedruco Decl.) Defendant's objections are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Carol Karen #8189.
